conflicts.explanation: When combined, Vitamin C and Niacinamide can form niacin which may cause temporary skin flushing in some people.
conflicts.recommendation: Use Vitamin C in the morning and Niacinamide in the evening, or use a stabilized Vitamin C derivative instead.

conflicts.explanation: Both require very low pH to work. Using together increases irritation risk significantly without added benefit.
conflicts.recommendation: Use one in the morning and one in the evening.

conflicts.explanation: Both are strong actives that increase cell turnover. Combined use causes severe irritation, redness and peeling.
conflicts.recommendation: Never use in the same routine. Use Retinol at night and Glycolic Acid on alternate nights.

conflicts.explanation: Both increase skin sensitivity and cell turnover. Combined use leads to severe dryness and irritation.
Benzoyl Peroxide oxidizes Retinol, making it completely ineffective. Wastes both products.
conflicts.recommendation: Never use together. Use Benzoyl Peroxide in the morning and Retinol at night.

conflicts.explanation: Both are strong actives. Combined use significantly increases dryness, irritation and barrier damage.
conflicts.recommendation: Use on alternate days or different times of day.

conflicts.explanation: Combining AHA and BHA doubles exfoliation, leading to over-exfoliation and a damaged skin barrier.
conflicts.recommendation: Use one at a time. Alternate between days.

conflicts.explanation: Vitamin C works at pH below 3.5 while Retinol works best at higher pH. Using together reduces efficacy of both.
conflicts.recommendation: Use Vitamin C in the morning and Retinol at night.

conflicts.explanation: Glycolic Acid temporarily raises skin pH when Niacinamide is present, reducing AHA exfoliation efficacy.
conflicts.recommendation: Apply Glycolic Acid first, wait 30 minutes, then apply Niacinamide.

conflicts.explanation: Salicylic Acid can break down Retinol and combined irritation is severe, especially for Indian skin in humid climates.
conflicts.recommendation: Never use in the same routine.
